channel.title: Java on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Java on yexca'Blog

item.title: Installing Java 8 (Oracle JDK) on Fedora
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash Intro Fedora comes with Java, but it’s OpenJDK. Sometimes, you just need Oracle’s version. Download Head to the official site to download: Java Downloads | Oracle (Requires login to download) Find java8-Linux, then grab the x64 Compressed Archive (that’s the 64-bit compressed package). As of writing, the file was named jdk-8u341-linux-x64.tar.gz. Move to Directory First, create a Java directory under /usr/local. 1 sudo mkdir …
